title: EARLY ASSESSMENT AND SUPPORT ALLIANCE - 211info
text: MARION COUNTY TRANSITIONAL AGE YOUTH SERVICES EARLY ASSESSMENT AND SUPPORT ALLIANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides rapid identification, support, assessment and treatment for teenagers and young adults who are experiencing early signs of psychosis. Services include education about psychosis; medication along with medication monitoring; individual and group counseling; multi-family groups; occupational therapy; family education and support; vocational training and life-skills coaching; 24/7 crisis services; connections to community resources. 3878 Beverly Avenue NE Salem, OR 97305 Eligibility: Ages 12-30 who have had a first episode of psychosis within the last 12 months or who are experiencing early at-risk symptoms for psychosis and their families Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (503) 576-4600 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 576-4600 Referrals (503) 576-4690 INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for intake FEES: Accepts OHP and private insurances. Sliding scale fees available for uninsured patients. DOCUMENTS: Insurance information, if applicable. Proof of income if applying for sliding scale fees. LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: Marion County SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Services: Early Intervention for Mental Illness If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. MARION COUNTY TRANSITIONAL AGE YOUTH SERVICES
